_description: Synthetic rqlite behavioral fixtures derived from the Python _rqlite_db.py implementation and the rqlite HTTP API contract. These are not captured from a live rqlite deployment (none at oracle capture). They describe required behavior that fabric-store-rqlite must implement.

scenarios.description: Atomic claim: UPDATE tasks SET status='claimed', worker_id=? WHERE id=? AND status='queued'. Single statement, no transaction. Returns rowcount=1 on success, 0 on race.
scenarios.sql: UPDATE tasks SET status = 'claimed', worker_id = ?, claimed_at = ? WHERE id = ? AND status = 'queued'
scenarios.on_rowcount_0: another runner claimed first — retry with next candidate

scenarios.description: Linearizable audit append: read tail, compute hash, INSERT with UNIQUE constraint on event_id_hash. Conflict = another writer appended first — retry from new tail.
scenarios.read_sql: SELECT event_id_hash FROM audit_event ORDER BY seq DESC LIMIT 1
scenarios.insert_sql: INSERT INTO audit_event (event_id_hash, prev_event_id_hash, kind, task_id, payload_json, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
scenarios.conflict_behavior: UNIQUE constraint on event_id_hash catches concurrent appends. Retry: re-read tail, recompute hash, re-insert.

scenarios.description: rqlite non-leader node returns HTTP 301/302 redirect to leader. Client must follow up to 3 times before treating as error.
rqlite cluster loses quorum (>= half nodes down). Writes return 503 Service Unavailable. Hub must surface audit-failure-profile=required if audit is required.
scenarios.required_hub_behavior: if audit_profile=required, reject new dispatches; if best_effort, log degraded and continue

scenarios.description: All timestamps must be explicit UTC strings, not datetime('now'). rqlite may run on nodes in different timezones.
